title: What detached HEAD means for new commits
description: When HEAD points directly at a commit instead of a branch, new commits are reachable only by hash and are lost once you check out elsewhere.
body: Use when git warns about a detached HEAD after checking out a tag or raw commit. Do not commit substantial work while detached without a branch. In this state HEAD references a commit, not a branch ref, so commits you make are unreferenced and become garbage-collection candidates after you switch away. Inspect with git status, which names the state explicitly. Validation signals include creating a branch with git switch -c before leaving, so the commits gain a permanent ref. Risk boundary: leaving detached HEAD without a branch risks silent loss of work, so attach a ref before checking out anything else.
